title: Cosmetic Oil on Polyester
timeEstimate: 20–60 minutes; powder + Dawn × 2 (silicone barrier), polyester locks oil under heat
stainChemistry: Cosmetic oils on polyester layer two complications. The oil itself is botanical + silicone + essential oils — silicone is the slick water-repellent film that resists single-pass cleaning. Polyester adds OLEOPHILIC affinity (grabs the oil tight) and heat-sensitivity (hot water sets the oil into the fiber, hot dryer locks any silicone residue and yellows the polyester over time). The recurring traps: hot water (sets oil + yellows polyester), hot dryer (locks silicone + permanent yellow), and skipping the second Dawn pass (silicone re-deposits from wash water). Polyester typically needs 2 Dawn passes minimum for silicone-heavy serums.
whyThisWorks: Powder preload pulls bulk oil up before water spreads it. Generous Dawn breaks through the silicone barrier and emulsifies the botanical oil. Second Dawn pass clears residual silicone — silicone re-deposits from wash water unless fully removed. Cool wash plus AIR-DRY locks nothing. The honest cap on polyester: hot-dried cosmetic oil yellowing is often a permanent shadow because polyester yellows under heat once oil is in the fiber.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): cosmetic oils combine triglyceride botanical oils + silicones (dimethicone, cyclomethicone) + essential oils. Silicones are hydrophobic and form a water-repellent film. Polyester is hydrophobic and oleophilic — silicone bonds particularly well. Pro uses POG / petroleum ether for silicone phase + amyl acetate for stubborn film + NSD.
defaultAssumption: Face serum / body oil / hair oil drip on a polyester shirt or athletic-wear. Fresh = real shot at full removal with powder + Dawn × 2. Hot-dried = often permanent yellow shadow. Performance / specialty / dry-clean-only polyester = pro from minute one.

spottingProtocol.technique: Pull bulk oil up before water
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cover the spot with cornstarch or baking soda. Press gently. 20–30 min absorb. Soft-brush off.
Generous Dawn directly on the spot. Soft toothbrush 10 min. Polyester is oleophilic — extended dwell needed. Cool damp-cloth rinse.
Touch with a clean fingertip. Slick or oily feel = repeat Dawn. Polyester typically needs 2 Dawn passes for silicone-heavy serums. Without the second pass, silicone re-deposits from wash water.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Washing machine, cool water, drying rack
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cool machine wash with normal detergent. SKIP fabric softener (coats fiber, blocks future cleaning). AIR-DRY only. Inspect dry. NEVER hot dryer until cleared — locks oil and yellows polyester.

steps: 1. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 20–30 min, soft-brush off.
2. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
3. Repeat Dawn pass — polyester needs 2 typically for silicone.
4. Cool wash + AIR-DRY. No softener, no dryer until cleared.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets cosmetic oil into polyester and yellows over time
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets oil + yellows polyester
NEVER fabric softener — coats fiber, blocks future cleaning
Powder preload BEFORE water — water-first spreads oil
Polyester is oleophilic — Dawn × 2 typical for silicone-heavy serums
Honest cap: hot-dried yellowing on polyester is often permanent

homeSolutions.agent: Polyester Loves Cosmetic Oil — Two Hard Rules
homeSolutions.instruction: Cosmetic oils on polyester combine two challenges: silicone (slick water-repellent film) plus polyester being oleophilic (grabs oil tight). The fix is the powder + Dawn × 2 sequence — without the second Dawn pass, silicone re-deposits from wash water. Two hard rules: NO hot water (sets the oil and yellows polyester) and NO hot dryer (locks silicone permanently and turns the yellow shadow permanent over time).

homeSolutions.agent: Cornstarch or Baking Soda — 20 to 30 Minutes
homeSolutions.instruction: Cover the spot with cornstarch or baking soda. Press gently. 20 to 30 minutes. Soft-brush off. Most surface oil comes off this way without water.

Apply a generous amount of Dawn dish soap directly to the spot. Work in with a soft toothbrush for 10 minutes. Polyester needs longer dwell than cotton because it grabs oil tight. Cool damp-cloth rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: Test for Slick / Oily Feel — Repeat If Yes
homeSolutions.instruction: Touch the spot with a clean fingertip. Still slick or oily? Repeat the Dawn step. Silicone-heavy serums typically need 2 Dawn passes on polyester — silicone re-deposits from wash water without full removal first.

Cool machine wash with normal detergent. SKIP fabric softener — it coats the fiber and blocks any future cleaning if buildup recurs. AIR-DRY only. Inspect in daylight before any dryer. Faint yellow shadow = repeat the Dawn pass. NEVER hot dryer until cleared — heat at this step locks residual oil and silicone permanently.

steps: 1. NO hot water. NO hot dryer until cleared. NO fabric softener.
2. Cornstarch / baking soda preload 20–30 min, soft-brush off.
3. Generous Dawn pretreat, cool, 10 min, soft toothbrush.
4. Repeat Dawn pass if oily/slick feel remains (2 passes typical).
6. AIR-DRY + daylight inspect. No dryer until cleared.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if hot water or hot dryer was already used (often permanent yellow shadow on polyester). Stop on performance / specialty / dry-clean-only polyester (pro). Stop after 3 home cycles plateau.
warnings: NEVER hot water — sets oil + yellows polyester
NEVER fabric softener — coats fiber, blocks cleaning
Polyester is oleophilic — 2 Dawn passes typical
Aged hot-dried yellow is often permanent

neverDo: Never hot water on cosmetic oil + polyester
Never fabric softener after cosmetic oil
Never skip the second Dawn pass on silicone-heavy serums
fiberSensitivities: Polyester is oleophilic — grabs cosmetic oil tightly, needs 2 Dawn passes
Polyester yellows under heat over time when oil is in the fiber
Performance / specialty polyester (compression, technical): pro — coatings react unpredictably
Athletic wear with fabric softener buildup: strip softener first or expect plateau

materialWarnings: Powder preload before water — water-first spreads oil
Polyester loves cosmetic oil — Dawn × 2 typical
No hot water / no hot dryer — polyester yellows under heat with oil
No fabric softener — coats fiber, blocks future cleaning

escalation.when: Hot water or hot dryer already used (often permanent yellow on polyester). Performance / specialty / dry-clean-only polyester. 3 home cycles plateau. Aged hot-dried buildup over months.
escalation.whatToTell: "Cosmetic oil on [white / dyed / athletic / specialty] polyester, [silicone-heavy serum / body oil / hair oil], age [fresh / hours / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [cornstarch preload + Dawn × 2 + cool wash + air-dry]. No hot, no softener, no dryer. [Residual yellow shadow / silicone re-deposit / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with solvent spotting capability for silicone-heavy cosmetic oils on polyester

contextQuestions: Face serum, body oil, hair oil, massage oil, or primer?
Silicone-heavy (smooth slick feel) or pure botanical oil?
White, dyed, athletic, or specialty polyester?
Fresh, hours old, or hot-dried already?
Has fabric softener been used in past washes?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Silicone is the entire challenge — silicone-heavy serums need 2 Dawn passes on polyester minimum. Pure botanical oils (jojoba, argan, marula, coconut) without silicone are easier — single Dawn pass usually sufficient. Athletic-wear fabric softener buildup is the hidden plateau cause. Performance polyester (compression, technical) goes to pro because coatings react unpredictably.
